scope.area: installer or setup rerun under the current single-main-process PM2 fork baseline, including smoke, stop, uninstall, clear, and lifecycle reporting
coverage: disposable installer clone flow from a local bare snapshot
single-fork PM2 runtime verification with `exec_mode: "fork"` and `instances: 1`
strict loopback smoke validation over `https://127.0.0.1` with `curl -sk --max-time 1`
cold first-hit versus warm repeated route timing
comparison against the earlier failed rerun, the latest successful lifecycle report, and the latest single-fork latency report

summary.highLevelVerdict: The March 23, 2026 rerun completed the disposable installer flow, scaffolded `www.fakedomain.com`, preserved the expected single-fork PM2 baseline, passed the full loopback smoke matrix for both `fakedomain.com` and `www.fakedomain.com`, stopped cleanly, uninstalled cleanly, and cleared preserved data cleanly. This run recovered the earlier null-cookie session regression: static, controller, and session routes are back to `200`, PM2 error output stayed empty, and the cold-versus-warm timing split again matches the expected pattern of one slower first controller hit followed by stable warm responses in the roughly 5.7 ms to 10.5 ms range.

runtimeTopology.warmSessionMethod: One warmup request created and reused a cookie jar before the repeated `/session` sample set.

commands.snapshotPrep: Create `/tmp/ehecoatl-lifecycle-2026-03-23-155847/source`, rsync the working tree into it while excluding `.git`, `.vscode`, `app/node_modules`, and `app/_log`, commit disposable snapshot `dade6e5`, clone it as `/tmp/ehecoatl-lifecycle-2026-03-23-155847/remote.git`, and run the copied launcher from `/tmp/ehecoatl-lifecycle-2026-03-23-155847/launcher`.
commands.smokeMatrix: Issue one strict local request for `/`, `/htm/index.htm`, `/htm/cached.htm`, `/hello`, and `/session` with both `Host: fakedomain.com` and `Host: www.fakedomain.com` using `curl -sk --max-time 1`.
commands.warmSeries: For `www.fakedomain.com`, issue one warmup request and then 10 repeated requests for `/`, `/htm/index.htm`, and `/hello`; for `/session`, reuse a cookie jar after one warmup request and record 10 repeated samples.

details: The lifecycle used a disposable source snapshot, a local bare git remote, and a copied launcher directory so `installer.sh` followed the true clone-to-`/opt/ehecoatl` path.
The authoring checkout remained available after uninstall so the explicit clear step could still run.
Pre-run checks confirmed `/opt/ehecoatl`, `/etc/opt/ehecoatl`, `/var/opt/ehecoatl`, `/srv/opt/ehecoatl`, and the root PM2 `Ehecoatl` list were clean before the rerun.
The installer completed successfully against the disposable local bare remote and setup scaffolded `www.fakedomain.com` automatically from the `fakedomain.com` prompt answer.
The installed copy preserved the current runtime baseline with `exec_mode: "fork"` and `instances: 1` in `/opt/ehecoatl/app/ecosystem.config.js`.

Observed ownership examples matched the current root-group permission model: `/etc/opt/ehecoatl` as `ehecoatl:root 2775`, `/etc/opt/ehecoatl/install-meta.env` as `ehecoatl:root 640`, `/var/opt/ehecoatl/tenants/fakedomain.com` as `ehecoatl:root 2775`, `/var/opt/ehecoatl/tenants/fakedomain.com/www` as `e_tenant_www.fakedomain.com:root 2755`, and host `config.json` as `e_tenant_www.fakedomain.com:root 640`.
`sudo npm run start` returned successfully and PM2 launched one root `fork` instance named `Ehecoatl`.
The first successful loopback smoke response arrived 53 ms after probing began and returned `302` for `GET /` with `Host: www.fakedomain.com`.

All checked routes passed with `--max-time 1` for both `Host: fakedomain.com` and `Host: www.fakedomain.com`: `/` returned `302`, `/htm/index.htm` and `/htm/cached.htm` returned `200` HTML, `/hello` returned `200` JSON, and `/session` returned `200` JSON with `sessionId` and `sessionData`.

The cold first-hit controller sample for `Host: fakedomain.com` on `/hello` took 317.581 ms while PM2 was launching `tenant_www.fakedomain.com`; once warm, the same route stabilized around 8.168 ms to 10.243 ms.

PM2 output showed manager and engine ready before tenant auto-spawn began, `tenant_www.fakedomain.com` launched at `2026-03-23T16:00:03.541Z`, reached ready at `2026-03-23T16:00:03.846Z`, and `pm2-err-0.log` remained empty during the smoke pass.
The uninstall script removed `/opt/ehecoatl`, removed `/usr/local/bin/ehecoatl`, and removed `/etc/opt/ehecoatl/install-meta.env`.
The expected preserved data trees under `/etc/opt/ehecoatl`, `/var/opt/ehecoatl`, and `/srv/opt/ehecoatl` remained present immediately after uninstall.
`setup/purge-data.sh` removed `/etc/opt/ehecoatl`, `/var/opt/ehecoatl`, and `/srv/opt/ehecoatl` successfully after the required `PURGE` confirmation.
After the clear step, `/opt/ehecoatl`, `/etc/opt/ehecoatl`, `/var/opt/ehecoatl`, and `/srv/opt/ehecoatl` were all absent.
The root PM2 process list was empty after the clear step, so no manual PM2 cleanup was needed.

requiredChecks.item: A disposable execution target is chosen so uninstall does not destroy the authoring workspace
requiredChecks.note: A disposable source snapshot, bare remote, and separate launcher were used.

requiredChecks.item: The rerun baseline still uses PM2 `fork` mode with one main instance
requiredChecks.note: The installed `/opt/ehecoatl/app/ecosystem.config.js` still declared `exec_mode: "fork"` and `instances: 1`, and PM2 reported one `fork` instance online.

requiredChecks.item: Installer or setup completes and scaffolds the `tenant-minimal` host for `www.fakedomain.com`
requiredChecks.note: Installer and setup completed, and the first-tenant scaffold for `fakedomain.com` created the expected `www` host.

requiredChecks.item: Loopback smoke proves `GET /`, `GET /htm/index.htm`, `GET /htm/cached.htm`, `GET /hello`, and `GET /session` with `curl --max-time 1` while sending `Host: fakedomain.com` or `Host: www.fakedomain.com`
requiredChecks.note: All checked routes succeeded within the `--max-time 1` limit for both host headers.

requiredChecks.item: Runtime stop completes without hanging or supervised-child teardown drift
requiredChecks.note: Stop completed successfully and local loopback refused connections immediately afterward.

requiredChecks.item: Uninstall removes the installed project, CLI, and install metadata while leaving runtime data present until the explicit clear step
requiredChecks.note: `/opt/ehecoatl`, `/usr/local/bin/ehecoatl`, and `install-meta.env` were absent immediately after uninstall, while `/etc/opt/ehecoatl`, `/var/opt/ehecoatl`, and `/srv/opt/ehecoatl` remained.

requiredChecks.item: The clear step removes the preserved `/etc/opt/ehecoatl`, `/var/opt/ehecoatl`, and `/srv/opt/ehecoatl` trees and leaves no PM2 residue
requiredChecks.note: All preserved data trees were absent after `purge-data.sh` and the root PM2 list was empty.

requiredChecks.item: A new `report/performance` artifact is written for the full install, smoke, stop, uninstall, and clear lifecycle
requiredChecks.note: This report is the new lifecycle artifact for the rerun.

details: The March 23, 2026 15:33 rerun failed the smoke phase because non-redirect routes returned `500` from the null-cookie session regression.
This rerun recovered that regression: static, controller, and session routes are back to successful responses, and PM2 error output stayed empty during the smoke pass.
The March 23, 2026 13:16 lifecycle report passed install, smoke, uninstall, and purge end to end under the earlier code state.
This rerun reproduces that successful lifecycle outcome on the current revision while refreshing the evidence with the latest helper split and current timing samples.
The March 23, 2026 13:43 latency report showed warm redirect, static, controller, and session routes succeeding in low double-digit milliseconds or better under the same single-fork baseline.
This rerun shows the same overall shape: one slower cold controller sample during tenant spawn and warm repeated route timings in the same rough 5.7 ms to 10.5 ms band.

nextAction.summary: The current revision is validated for the tested single-fork installer, smoke, uninstall, and clear lifecycle.
recommendedFocus: Use this rerun as the new clean lifecycle reference for future regressions under the single-fork baseline.
Keep the null-cookie session tests and helper-split tests in the regular focused test set so future adapter changes do not reintroduce the recovered failure.
